name: interview-me
description_raw: Extracts what the user actually wants instead of what they think they should want. Achieves this through one-question-at-a-time interview until ~95% confidence about the underlying intent. Use when an ask is underspecified, when the user explicitly invokes, or when you catch yourself silently filling in ambiguous requirements before any plan, spec, or code exists.
description_normalized: Closes the gap between what users ask for and what they actually want via one-question-at-a-time interview before any plan or code begins.

frontmatter_yaml.name: interview-me

Use when an ask is underspecified ("build me X" without "for whom" or "why now"), when the user explicitly invokes ("interview me", "grill me", "are we sure?", "stress-test my thinking"), or when you catch yourself silently filling in ambiguous requirements before any plan, spec, or code exists.

RVETS = React 19 + Vite 7 + Express 5 + TypeScript + Socket.io — npm workspaces monorepo with client/, server/, shared/ packages. Trigger on: "create a new AppyStack app", "scaffold a new RVETS project", "new AppyStack project", "create-appystack", "new full-stack app with AppyStack", "spin up an AppyStack". Inside an existing AppyStack project, the "recipe" skill handles feature scaffolding instead.

name: dark-factory-catalog

Supports a requirements mode (trigger: "requirements tour", "pre-rebuild tour", "capture requirements for...") that adds role tagging, per-screen requirements fields, user flow mapping, design system observations, and a REQUIREMENTS.md export — the handover artifact for UX designers and developers doing a rebuild. Use when the user wants to document an app visually, generate a screenshot walkthrough, capture all screens for mockups or docs, describe existing screenshots, or run a screenshot tour of AngelEye, FliHub, or any other web app.
description_normalized: Automates full click-through screenshot documentation of web applications.

name: skill-miner
description_raw: Mines David's past Claude Code session transcripts (JSONL files in ~/.claude/projects/) for recurring patterns and promotes them into skill candidates that would improve his workflow. Use when David says "skill-miner", "mine my skills", "skill mining", "any skill candidates", "what should be a skill", "find skill patterns", "log this for skill-miner", "any new skills emerging", "skill miner scan". Two modes — scan (retrospective batch over recent JSONLs; default 7-day window) and log (hot-capture mid-session when David notices friction worth flagging). Patterns accumulate at ~/.claude/skill-miner/candidates.md, decay after 60+ days unseen, and promote to skill-stub drafts in promoted/ at 3+ instances within 90 days. Scoring is friction × frequency × cost, not raw repetition. Retrospective counterpart to AngelEye's live ingestion.

frontmatter_yaml.description: Multi-reviewer quality check for knowledge work. Runs strategic alignment and data accuracy reviewers on plans, briefs, and strategy docs.

name: kw:work
description_raw: Execute a knowledge work plan. Break it into tasks, do the work, and track what happened. Use after planning to actually produce the deliverables.

frontmatter_yaml.name: kw:work
frontmatter_yaml.description: Execute a knowledge work plan. Break it into tasks, do the work, and track what happened. Use after planning to actually produce the deliverables.

name: kw:compound

frontmatter_yaml.name: kw:compound
frontmatter_yaml.description: Extract and save learnings from a completed knowledge work session. Saves to docs/knowledge/ so future plans automatically find them.

name: knowledge-base-researcher

description_normalized: Greps docs/knowledge/ by keyword and YAML tag to retrieve past learnings, grouping results as Directly Relevant / Tangentially Relevant / Not Found, and flags stale or low-confidence entries.

frontmatter_yaml.name: knowledge-base-researcher
frontmatter_yaml.description: Search docs/knowledge/ for saved learnings that are relevant to the current topic. Returns insights, corrections, playbooks, and patterns from past sessions. This is the agent that closes the compounding loop.

description_normalized: Runs after kw:compound extracts a new learning; scans docs/knowledge/ for contradicted, superseded, or complementary entries and returns update/remove/keep recommendations without writing any files.

description_normalized: Reviews plans and briefs for strategic soundness across 7 criteria (goal clarity, falsifiability, metrics, scope, resources, consistency, opportunity cost), returning P1/P2/P3 findings grouped by severity.

name: data-accuracy-reviewer
description_raw: Use this agent when you need to verify that numbers, metrics, and data claims in knowledge work artifacts are accurate, sourced, and fresh. This includes checking that every number has a cited source, comparison baselines are explicit, data matches canonical definitions, and known caveats are acknowledged.
description_normalized: Verifies every data claim in a knowledge work artifact against 7 criteria (source citation, baselines, canonical definitions, freshness, caveats, hardcoded vs live, baseline appropriateness), returning P1/P2/P3 findings.

name: flox-environments
description_raw: Create reproducible, cross-platform development environments with Flox — a declarative environment manager built on Nix. ALWAYS use this skill when the user needs to: set up a project with system-level dependencies (compilers, databases, native libraries like openssl, libvips, BLAS, LAPACK); configure reproducible toolchains for Python, Node.js, Rust, Go, C/C++, Java, Ruby, Elixir, PHP, or any language; manage environments that must work identically across macOS and Linux; pin exact package versions for a team; run local services (PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka) alongside development tools; onboard new developers with a single command; or solve 'works on my machine' problems. Especially valuable for AI-assisted and vibe coding — Flox lets agents install tools into a project-scoped environment without sudo, system pollution, or sandbox restrictions, and the resulting environment is committed to the repo so anyone can reproduce it instantly. Use this skill even if the user doesn't mention Flox — if they describe needing reproducible, declarative, cross-platform dev environments with system packages, this is the right tool. Also use when the user mentions .flox/, manifest.toml, flox activate, or FloxHub.

name: token-budget-advisor
description_raw: Offers the user an informed choice about how much response depth to consume before answering. Use this skill when the user explicitly wants to control response length, depth, or token budget. TRIGGER when: "token budget", "token count", "token usage", "token limit", "response length", "answer depth", "short version", "brief answer", "detailed answer", "exhaustive answer", "respuesta corta vs larga", "cuántos tokens", "ahorrar tokens", "responde al 50%", "dame la versión corta", "quiero controlar cuánto usas", or clear variants where the user is explicitly asking to control answer size or depth. DO NOT TRIGGER when: user has already specified a level in the current session (maintain it), the request is clearly a one-word answer, or "token" refers to auth/session/payment tokens rather than response size.

name: academic-verify
description_raw: Verify a research claim or academic citation by tracing it through publication → methodology → raw data → independent replication. Routes through perplexity-research for the actual web lookup, then formats results as a citation-checked brain page. Use when a book/article/conversation cites a study and you want to confirm the claim is real, replicated, and accurately characterized.
description_normalized: Verify a research claim or academic citation by tracing it through publication → methodology → raw data → independent replication.

name: archive-crawler
description_raw: Universal archivist for personal file archives (Dropbox/B2/Gmail-takeout/local-mount/hard-drive-dump). Filters for high-value content (the user's own writing, ideas, relationships) and surfaces it interactively. REFUSES TO RUN without an explicit gbrain.yml archive-crawler.scan_paths: allow-list.
description_normalized: Universal archivist for personal file archives (Dropbox/B2/Gmail-takeout/local-mount/hard-drive-dump).

name: article-enrichment
description_raw: Transform raw article text dumps in the brain into structured pages with executive summary, verbatim quotes, key insights, why-it-matters, and cross-references. Replaces walls-of-text with quotable, actionable brain pages.
description_normalized: Transform raw article text dumps in the brain into structured pages with executive summary, verbatim quotes, key insights, why-it-matters, and cross-references.

name: ask-user
description_raw: Reusable pattern for presenting the user with explicit choices and gating execution until they respond. Used by other skills when a decision point requires human input before proceeding. Platform-agnostic — works on Telegram (inline buttons), Discord, CLI, or any agent with a message tool.
description_normalized: Reusable pattern for presenting the user with explicit choices and gating execution until they respond.

name: book-mirror
description_raw: Take any book (EPUB/PDF), produce a personalized chapter-by-chapter analysis with two-column tables. Left column preserves the chapter content; right column maps every idea to the reader's actual life using brain context. Output is a single brain page at media/books/<slug>-personalized.md plus an optional PDF via brain-pdf.
description_normalized: Take any book (EPUB/PDF), produce a personalized chapter-by-chapter analysis with two-column tables.

name: brain-ops
description_raw: Brain knowledge base operations. The core read/write cycle: brain-first lookup, read-enrich-write loop, source attribution, ambient enrichment, back-linking. Read this before any brain interaction.
description_normalized: Brain knowledge base operations.

name: brain-pdf
description_raw: Generate a publication-quality PDF from any brain page via the gstack make-pdf binary. Strips YAML frontmatter, sanitizes emoji, applies running headers and page numbers. Brain page is always the source of truth; PDF is a rendering.
description_normalized: Generate a publication-quality PDF from any brain page via the gstack make-pdf binary.

name: briefing
description_raw: Compile daily briefing with meeting context, active deals, and citation tracking.
description_normalized: Compile daily briefing with meeting context, active deals, and citation tracking.

name: citation-fixer
description_raw: Audit and fix citation formatting across brain pages. Ensures every fact has an inline [Source: ...] citation matching the standard format. Extended in v0.25.1: scans for broken tweet/post references that lack actual URLs and resolves them via the host's X / Twitter API integration.
description_normalized: Audit and fix citation formatting across brain pages.

name: cold-start
description_raw: Day-one data bootstrapping for a new brain. Sequences the highest-leverage data sources to go from empty brain to useful brain in one session. Uses ClawVisor for safe credential handling — the agent never holds raw API keys. Covers Gmail import, calendar sync, contacts seeding, X/Twitter archive, conversation imports, and file archives.
description_normalized: Day-one data bootstrapping for a new brain.

name: concept-synthesis
description_raw: Deduplicate and synthesize raw concept stubs into a tiered intellectual map (T1 Canon to T4 Riff), tracing idea evolution across sources over time. Transforms thousands of raw concept pages into a curated intellectual fingerprint.
description_normalized: Deduplicate and synthesize raw concept stubs into a tiered intellectual map (T1 Canon to T4 Riff), tracing idea evolution across sources over time.

name: cron-scheduler
description_raw: Schedule management with staggering, quiet hours, and wake-up override. Validates schedules, prevents collisions, and gates delivery during quiet hours.
description_normalized: Schedule management with staggering, quiet hours, and wake-up override.

name: cross-modal-review
description_raw: Quality gate via second model. Spawn a different AI model to review work before committing. Includes refusal routing: if one model refuses, switch silently to the next. Extended in v0.25.1 with structured review-mode gating (when to invoke vs not) and a Codex code-review handoff for the diff-review case.
description_normalized: Quality gate via second model.

name: daily-task-manager
description_raw: Task lifecycle management. Add, complete, defer, remove, and review tasks. Maintains a running task list as a brain page.
description_normalized: Task lifecycle management.

name: daily-task-prep
description_raw: Morning preparation. Calendar lookahead, meeting context loading, open threads from yesterday, active task review. Extends briefing with actionable prep.
description_normalized: Morning preparation.

name: data-research
description_raw: Structured data research: search sources, extract structured data, archive raw sources, maintain canonical tracker pages, deduplicate. Parameterized via YAML recipes for investor updates, donations, company updates, or any email-to-structured-data pipeline.
description_normalized: Structured data research: search sources, extract structured data, archive raw sources, maintain canonical tracker pages, deduplicate.

name: enrich
description_raw: Enrich brain pages with tiered enrichment protocol. Creates and updates person/company pages with compiled truth, timeline, and cross-links. Use when a new entity is mentioned or an existing page needs updating.
description_normalized: Enrich brain pages with tiered enrichment protocol.

name: frontmatter-guard
description_raw: Validate and auto-repair YAML frontmatter on brain pages. Catches malformed pages before they enter the brain (missing closing ---, nested quotes, slug mismatches, null bytes, empty frontmatter, YAML parse failures). Wraps the gbrain frontmatter CLI for agent-driven workflows.
description_normalized: Validate and auto-repair YAML frontmatter on brain pages.

name: functional-area-resolver
description_raw: Compress an agent's routing file (RESOLVER.md or AGENTS.md) by converting granular skill-per-row tables into functional-area dispatchers. Each area lists sub-skills in a '(dispatcher for: ...)' clause. The LLM reads one area entry and routes to the correct sub-skill. Proven via held-out A/B eval: dispatcher pattern outperforms naive pipe-table compression.
description_normalized: Compress an agent's routing file (RESOLVER.

name: idea-ingest
description_raw: Ingest links, articles, tweets, and ideas into the brain. Fetch content, save to brain with analysis, create author people page, and cross-link. Use when the user shares a link or says 'read this', 'save this', 'think about this'.
description_normalized: Ingest links, articles, tweets, and ideas into the brain.

name: ingest
description_raw: Route content to specialized ingestion skills. Detects input type and delegates.
description_normalized: Route content to specialized ingestion skills.

name: install
description_raw: Install GBrain (Deprecated). This skill has been replaced by the setup skill. See skills/setup/SKILL.md.
description_normalized: Install GBrain (Deprecated).

name: maintain
description_raw: Brain health checks: back-link enforcement, citation audit, filing validation, stale info detection, orphan pages, and benchmarks. Use when asked to check brain health, run maintenance, or audit quality.
description_normalized: Brain health checks: back-link enforcement, citation audit, filing validation, stale info detection, orphan pages, and benchmarks.

name: media-ingest
description_raw: Ingest video, audio, PDF, book, screenshot, and GitHub repo content into the brain. Multi-format handling with entity extraction and backlink propagation. Covers video-ingest, youtube-ingest, and book-ingest subtypes.
description_normalized: Ingest video, audio, PDF, book, screenshot, and GitHub repo content into the brain.

name: meeting-ingestion
description_raw: Ingest meeting transcripts into brain pages with attendee enrichment, entity propagation, and timeline merge. A meeting is NOT fully ingested until the enrich skill has processed every entity.
description_normalized: Ingest meeting transcripts into brain pages with attendee enrichment, entity propagation, and timeline merge.

name: migrate
description_raw: Universal migration from Obsidian, Notion, Logseq, markdown, CSV, JSON, Roam.
description_normalized: Universal migration from Obsidian, Notion, Logseq, markdown, CSV, JSON, Roam.

name: minion-orchestrator
description_raw: Unified Minions skill for both deterministic shell jobs and LLM subagent orchestration. Replaces the older gbrain-jobs routing intent. Use when: submitting gbrain jobs, shell/background tasks, spawning subagents, checking progress, steering running work, pausing/resuming, parallel fan-out. One durable, observable, steerable queue interface.
description_normalized: Unified Minions skill for both deterministic shell jobs and LLM subagent orchestration.

name: perplexity-research
description_raw: Brain-augmented web research. Sends brain context about a topic to Perplexity, which searches the web with citations and returns what is NEW vs what the brain already knows. Use for entity enrichment, current-state checks, deal monitoring, and freshness deltas. NOT for simple URL fetches (use web_fetch) or brain-only queries (use gbrain query).
description_normalized: Brain-augmented web research.

name: publish
description_raw: Share brain pages as beautiful password-protected HTML with zero LLM calls.
description_normalized: Share brain pages as beautiful password-protected HTML with zero LLM calls.

name: query
description_raw: Answer questions using the brain's knowledge with 3-layer search, synthesis, and citation propagation. Use when the user asks a question, wants a lookup, or needs information from the brain.
description_normalized: Answer questions using the brain's knowledge with 3-layer search, synthesis, and citation propagation.

name: repo-architecture
description_raw: Where new brain files go. Decision protocol for filing brain pages by primary subject, not by format or source. Reference for all brain-writing skills.
description_normalized: Where new brain files go.

name: reports
description_raw: Save and load timestamped reports. Keyword routing for fast lookup. Cron jobs save output as reports; the agent or user queries them by keyword.
description_normalized: Save and load timestamped reports.

name: setup
description_raw: Set up GBrain with auto-provision Supabase or PGLite, AGENTS.md injection, first import.
description_normalized: Set up GBrain with auto-provision Supabase or PGLite, AGENTS.

name: signal-detector
description_raw: Always-on ambient signal capture. Fires on every inbound message to detect original thinking and entity mentions. Spawn as a cheap sub-agent in parallel, never block the main response.
description_normalized: Always-on ambient signal capture.

name: skill-creator
description_raw: Create new skills following the GBrain conformance standard. Generates SKILL.md with frontmatter, Contract, Phases, Output Format, and Anti-Patterns. Checks MECE against existing skills. Updates manifest and resolver.
description_normalized: Create new skills following the GBrain conformance standard.

name: skillify
description_raw: The meta skill. Turn any raw feature into a properly-skilled, tested, resolvable unit of agent capability. Cross-modal eval is the recommended Phase 3 quality gate: 3 frontier models from different providers critique the output, you iterate to quality, THEN write tests that lock in the proven-good behavior.
description_normalized: The meta skill.

name: skillpack-check
description_raw: Run gbrain skillpack-check to produce an agent-readable JSON health report for the gbrain install. Wraps gbrain doctor + gbrain apply-migrations --list so a host agent can see at a glance whether the skillpack needs attention. Use when the user asks 'is gbrain healthy?', when a cron fires a morning check, or proactively when something seems off.
description_normalized: Run gbrain skillpack-check to produce an agent-readable JSON health report for the gbrain install.

name: smoke-test
description_raw: Post-restart smoke tests + auto-fix for gbrain and OpenClaw environments. Tests critical services, auto-fixes known issues, extensible via user-defined test scripts in ~/.gbrain/smoke-tests.d/*.sh.
description_normalized: Post-restart smoke tests + auto-fix for gbrain and OpenClaw environments.

name: soul-audit
description_raw: 6-phase interactive interview that generates the agent's identity (SOUL.md), user profile (USER.md), access control (ACCESS_POLICY.md), and operational cadence (HEARTBEAT.md). Re-runnable anytime to update any section.
description_normalized: 6-phase interactive interview that generates the agent's identity (SOUL.

name: strategic-reading
description_raw: Read a book, article, transcript, or case study through the lens of a specific strategic problem you're facing. Produces an applied playbook that maps the source onto the problem and gives short/medium/long-term recommendations. NOT for general book summaries.
description_normalized: Read a book, article, transcript, or case study through the lens of a specific strategic problem you're facing.

name: testing
description_raw: Skill validation framework PLUS daily test-suite health and regression intelligence. Validates skill conformance (frontmatter, manifest coverage, resolver coverage). Runs the project test suite in tiered phases (unit / evals / integration / system health), classifies failures, and produces a regression-aware report.
description_normalized: Skill validation framework PLUS daily test-suite health and regression intelligence.

name: voice-note-ingest
description_raw: Ingest a voice note with exact-phrasing preservation (never paraphrased). Routes content to originals/, concepts/, people/, companies/, ideas/, personal/, or voice-notes/ based on a decision tree. The user's exact words are the signal.
description_normalized: Ingest a voice note with exact-phrasing preservation (never paraphrased).

name: webhook-transforms
description_raw: Generic framework for converting external events (SMS, meetings, social mentions) into brain-ingestible signals. Define a transform function, register a webhook URL, and incoming events get processed through the brain pipeline.
description_normalized: Generic framework for converting external events (SMS, meetings, social mentions) into brain-ingestible signals.

name: calendar-to-brain
description_raw: [Recipe — integration playbook] Google Calendar events become searchable brain pages. Daily files with attendees, locations, and meeting prep context.
description_normalized: Integration recipe: Google Calendar events become searchable brain pages. Daily files with attendees, locations, and meeting prep context.

name: credential-gateway
description_raw: [Recipe — integration playbook] Secure access to Gmail, Google Calendar, and other Google services. ClawVisor (recommended) or direct Google OAuth.
description_normalized: Integration recipe: Secure access to Gmail, Google Calendar, and other Google services. ClawVisor (recommended) or direct Google OAuth.

name: email-to-brain
description_raw: [Recipe — integration playbook] Gmail messages flow into brain pages. Deterministic collector pulls emails, agent analyzes and enriches entities.
description_normalized: Integration recipe: Gmail messages flow into brain pages. Deterministic collector pulls emails, agent analyzes and enriches entities.

name: meeting-sync
description_raw: [Recipe — integration playbook] Meeting transcripts from Circleback auto-import into brain pages with attendee detection and entity propagation.
description_normalized: Integration recipe: Meeting transcripts from Circleback auto-import into brain pages with attendee detection and entity propagation.

name: ngrok-tunnel
description_raw: [Recipe — integration playbook] Fixed public URL for your brain (MCP server, voice agent, any service). One ngrok account, never changes.
description_normalized: Integration recipe: Fixed public URL for your brain (MCP server, voice agent, any service). One ngrok account, never changes.

name: restart-sweep
description_raw: [Recipe — integration playbook] Detect Telegram messages dropped during OpenClaw gateway restarts. Reads OpenClaw session state, alerts on aborted-mid-run sessions and (opt-in) suspicious silence gaps. Cooldown-gated so repeat detections don't spam.
description_normalized: Integration recipe: Detect Telegram messages dropped during OpenClaw gateway restarts. Reads OpenClaw session state, alerts on aborted-mid-run sessions and (opt-in) suspicious silence gaps. Cooldown-gated so repeat detections don't spam.

name: twilio-voice-brain
description_raw: [Recipe — integration playbook] Phone calls create brain pages via Twilio + voice pipeline + GBrain MCP. Two architectures -- OpenAI Realtime (turnkey) or DIY STT+LLM+TTS (full control). Callers talk, brain pages appear.
description_normalized: Integration recipe: Phone calls create brain pages via Twilio + voice pipeline + GBrain MCP. Two architectures -- OpenAI Realtime (turnkey) or DIY STT+LLM+TTS (full control). Callers talk, brain pages appear.

name: x-to-brain
description_raw: [Recipe — integration playbook] Twitter timeline, mentions, and keyword monitoring flow into brain pages. Tracks deletions, engagement velocity, OCR on images, and real-time alerts.
description_normalized: Integration recipe: Twitter timeline, mentions, and keyword monitoring flow into brain pages. Tracks deletions, engagement velocity, OCR on images, and real-time alerts.

name: gsd:add-backlog
description_raw: Add an idea to the backlog parking lot (999.x numbering). Add a backlog item to the roadmap using 999.x numbering. Backlog items are unsequenced ideas that aren't ready for active planning — they live outside the normal phase sequence and accumulate context over time.
description_normalized: Adds an unsequenced idea to the roadmap backlog using 999.x phase numbering.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:add-backlog
frontmatter_yaml.description: Add an idea to the backlog parking lot (999.x numbering)

name: gsd:add-phase
description_raw: Add phase to end of current milestone in roadmap. Handles: phase number calculation (next sequential integer), directory creation with slug generation, roadmap structure updates, STATE.md roadmap evolution tracking.
description_normalized: Appends a new sequential integer phase to the end of the current milestone roadmap.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:add-phase
frontmatter_yaml.description: Add phase to end of current milestone in roadmap

name: gsd:add-tests
description_raw: Generate tests for a completed phase based on UAT criteria and implementation. Generates unit and E2E tests for a completed phase, using its SUMMARY.md, CONTEXT.md, and VERIFICATION.md as specifications.
description_normalized: Generates unit and E2E tests for a completed phase using its planning artifacts as specifications.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:add-tests
frontmatter_yaml.description: Generate tests for a completed phase based on UAT criteria and implementation

name: gsd:add-todo
description_raw: Capture idea or task as todo from current conversation context. Handles: directory structure creation, content extraction from arguments or conversation, area inference from file paths, duplicate detection and resolution, todo file creation with frontmatter.
description_normalized: Captures an idea or task surfaced in a GSD session as a structured todo for later work.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:add-todo
frontmatter_yaml.description: Capture idea or task as todo from current conversation context

name: gsd:analyze-dependencies
description_raw: Analyze phase dependencies and suggest Depends on entries for ROADMAP.md. Analyzes the phase dependency graph for the current milestone: file overlap, semantic dependencies, data flow.
description_normalized: Analyzes the phase dependency graph and suggests ROADMAP.md Depends-on entries.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:analyze-dependencies
frontmatter_yaml.description: Analyze phase dependencies and suggest Depends on entries for ROADMAP.md

name: gsd:audit-milestone
description_raw: Audit milestone completion against original intent before archiving. Verifies milestone achieved its definition of done. Reads existing VERIFICATION.md files, aggregates tech debt and deferred gaps, then spawns integration checker for cross-phase wiring.
description_normalized: Audits milestone completion against requirements before archiving, spawning an integration checker for cross-phase verification.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:audit-milestone
frontmatter_yaml.description: Audit milestone completion against original intent before archiving

name: gsd:audit-uat
description_raw: Cross-phase audit of all outstanding UAT and verification items. Scans all phases for pending, skipped, blocked, and human_needed UAT items. Cross-references against codebase to detect stale documentation.
description_normalized: Scans all phases for outstanding UAT items and cross-references against the live codebase to detect stale documentation.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:audit-uat
frontmatter_yaml.description: Cross-phase audit of all outstanding UAT and verification items

name: gsd:autonomous
description_raw: Run all remaining phases autonomously — discuss→plan→execute per phase. Pauses only for user decisions (grey area acceptance, blockers, validation requests). Uses ROADMAP.md phase discovery and Skill() flat invocations for each phase command.
description_normalized: Runs all remaining milestone phases autonomously (discuss→plan→execute), pausing only for user decisions.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:autonomous
frontmatter_yaml.description: Run all remaining phases autonomously — discuss→plan→execute per phase

name: gsd:check-todos
description_raw: List pending todos and select one to work on. Handles: todo counting and listing with area filtering, interactive selection with full context loading, roadmap correlation checking, action routing (work now, add to phase, brainstorm, create phase), STATE.md updates.
description_normalized: Lists pending todos, allows selection, and routes to the appropriate action (work now, promote to phase, etc.).

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:check-todos
frontmatter_yaml.description: List pending todos and select one to work on

name: gsd:cleanup
description_raw: Archive accumulated phase directories from completed milestones into .planning/milestones/v{X.Y}-phases/. Use when .planning/phases/ has accumulated directories from past milestones.
description_normalized: Archives completed milestone phase directories into a versioned archive folder.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:cleanup
frontmatter_yaml.description: Archive accumulated phase directories from completed milestones

name: gsd:complete-milestone
description_raw: Archive completed milestone and prepare for next version. Mark milestone complete, archive to milestones/, update ROADMAP.md and REQUIREMENTS.md. Creates historical record of shipped version.
description_normalized: Archives a completed milestone, updates planning files, and git-tags the historical record.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:complete-milestone
frontmatter_yaml.description: Archive completed milestone and prepare for next version

name: gsd:debug
description_raw: Systematic debugging with persistent state across context resets. Orchestrator gathers symptoms, spawns gsd-debugger agent, handles checkpoints, spawns continuations. --diagnose flag diagnoses only without fix.
description_normalized: Orchestrates systematic debugging by spawning a fresh-context gsd-debugger subagent with persistent state across context resets.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:debug
frontmatter_yaml.description: Systematic debugging with persistent state across context resets

name: gsd:discuss-phase
description_raw: Gather phase context through adaptive questioning before planning. Extracts implementation decisions that downstream agents need. --auto skips interactive questions, --chain chains to plan+execute, --power generates questions into file-based UI.
description_normalized: Gathers phase implementation decisions through adaptive questioning, producing CONTEXT.md consumed by researcher and planner.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:discuss-phase
frontmatter_yaml.description: Gather phase context through adaptive questioning before planning

name: gsd:do
description_raw: Route freeform text to the right GSD command automatically. Analyzes freeform natural language input and dispatches to the most appropriate GSD command. Never does the work itself.
description_normalized: Dispatches freeform natural language to the appropriate GSD slash command via intent routing.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:do
frontmatter_yaml.description: Route freeform text to the right GSD command automatically
frontmatter_yaml.argument-hint: <description of what you want to do>

name: gsd:docs-update
description_raw: Generate or update project documentation verified against the codebase. Each doc type is written by a gsd-doc-writer subagent that explores the codebase directly — no hallucinated paths, phantom endpoints, or stale signatures.
description_normalized: Generates up to 9 verified project documentation files using parallel gsd-doc-writer subagents that explore the codebase directly.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:docs-update
frontmatter_yaml.description: Generate or update project documentation verified against the codebase

name: gsd:execute-phase
description_raw: Execute all plans in a phase with wave-based parallelization. Orchestrator stays lean: discover plans, analyze dependencies, group into waves, spawn subagents, collect results. Each subagent loads the full execute-plan context.
description_normalized: Executes all phase plans in dependency-ordered parallel waves using fresh-context gsd-executor subagents.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:execute-phase
frontmatter_yaml.description: Execute all plans in a phase with wave-based parallelization

name: gsd:fast
description_raw: Execute a trivial task inline — no subagents, no planning overhead. For tasks too small to justify planning overhead: typo fixes, config changes, small refactors, forgotten commits, simple additions.
description_normalized: Executes trivial tasks directly in the current context without subagents or planning overhead.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:fast
frontmatter_yaml.description: Execute a trivial task inline — no subagents, no planning overhead

name: gsd:forensics
description_raw: Post-mortem investigation for failed GSD workflows — analyzes git history, artifacts, and state to diagnose what went wrong. Produces forensic report saved to .planning/forensics/.
description_normalized: Post-mortem investigation of failed GSD workflows by analyzing git history, planning artifacts, and filesystem state.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:forensics
frontmatter_yaml.description: Post-mortem investigation for failed GSD workflows — analyzes git history, artifacts, and state to diagnose what went wrong

name: gsd:health
description_raw: Diagnose planning directory health and optionally repair issues. Validates .planning/ directory integrity and reports actionable issues: missing files, invalid configurations, inconsistent state, and orphaned plans.
description_normalized: Diagnoses .planning/ directory integrity and optionally repairs issues like missing files and inconsistent state.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:health
frontmatter_yaml.description: Diagnose planning directory health and optionally repair issues

name: gsd:help
description_raw: Show available GSD commands and usage guide. Output ONLY the reference content. Do NOT add project-specific analysis, git status, next-step suggestions, or any commentary beyond the reference.
description_normalized: Displays the complete GSD command reference with strict no-commentary output constraint.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:help
frontmatter_yaml.description: Show available GSD commands and usage guide

name: gsd:insert-phase
description_raw: Insert urgent work as decimal phase (e.g., 72.1) between existing phases. Handle urgent work discovered during execution without renumbering entire roadmap.
description_normalized: Inserts a decimal-numbered urgent phase between existing integer phases without renumbering the roadmap.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:insert-phase
frontmatter_yaml.description: Insert urgent work as decimal phase (e.g., 72.1) between existing phases

name: gsd:join-discord
description_raw: Join the GSD Discord community. Displays the Discord invite link for the GSD community server.
description_normalized: Displays the GSD Discord community invite link.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:join-discord
frontmatter_yaml.description: Join the GSD Discord community

name: gsd:list-phase-assumptions
description_raw: Surface Claude's assumptions about a phase approach before planning. Analyzes a phase and presents assumptions about technical approach, implementation order, scope boundaries, risk areas, and dependencies.
description_normalized: Surfaces the agent's implicit assumptions about a phase before planning begins, enabling early course correction.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:list-phase-assumptions
frontmatter_yaml.description: Surface Claude's assumptions about a phase approach before planning

name: gsd:list-workspaces
description_raw: List all GSD workspaces with status overview.
description_normalized: Lists all GSD workspaces with their current status overview.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:list-workspaces
frontmatter_yaml.description: List all GSD workspaces with status overview

name: gsd:manager
description_raw: Single-terminal command center for managing a milestone. Shows a dashboard of all phases with visual status indicators, recommends optimal next actions, and dispatches work — discuss runs inline, plan/execute run as background agents.
description_normalized: Single-terminal milestone command center with phase dashboard and intelligent work dispatching.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:manager
frontmatter_yaml.description: Single-terminal command center for managing a milestone

name: gsd:map-codebase
description_raw: Analyze codebase with parallel mapper agents to produce .planning/codebase/ documents. Uses parallel gsd-codebase-mapper agents for tech, arch, quality, and concerns focus areas.
description_normalized: Analyzes the codebase using parallel gsd-codebase-mapper agents across four focus dimensions, writing structured analysis documents.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:map-codebase
frontmatter_yaml.description: Analyze codebase with parallel mapper agents to produce .planning/codebase/ documents
frontmatter_yaml.argument-hint: [optional: specific area to map, e.g., 'api' or 'auth']

name: gsd:milestone-summary
description_raw: Generate a comprehensive project summary from milestone artifacts for team onboarding and review. Reads completed milestone artifacts and produces a human-friendly overview of what was built, how, and why.
description_normalized: Generates a human-friendly milestone summary from planning artifacts for team onboarding and project review.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:milestone-summary
frontmatter_yaml.description: Generate a comprehensive project summary from milestone artifacts for team onboarding and review

name: gsd:new-milestone
description_raw: Start a new milestone cycle — update PROJECT.md and route to requirements. Brownfield equivalent of new-project. Gathers 'what's next', updates PROJECT.md, then runs requirements → roadmap cycle.
description_normalized: Starts a new milestone cycle on an existing project by gathering intent and routing to requirements and roadmap creation.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:new-milestone
frontmatter_yaml.description: Start a new milestone cycle — update PROJECT.md and route to requirements

name: gsd:new-project
description_raw: Initialize a new project with deep context gathering and PROJECT.md. Includes a Copilot runtime note for VS Code compatibility (vscode_askquestions equivalent to AskUserQuestion).
description_normalized: Initializes a new project with deep context gathering, requirements extraction, and PROJECT.md creation.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:new-project
frontmatter_yaml.description: Initialize a new project with deep context gathering and PROJECT.md

name: gsd:new-workspace
description_raw: Create an isolated workspace with repo copies and independent .planning/. Supports --strategy worktree|clone with --name, --repos, --path, --branch, --auto flags.
description_normalized: Creates an isolated GSD workspace with independent .planning/ via worktree or clone strategy.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:new-workspace
frontmatter_yaml.description: Create an isolated workspace with repo copies and independent .planning/

name: gsd:next
description_raw: Automatically advance to the next logical step in the GSD workflow. Reads STATE.md, ROADMAP.md, and phase directories to determine what comes next. Designed for rapid multi-project workflows.
description_normalized: Detects current project state and auto-advances to the next logical GSD workflow step.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:next
frontmatter_yaml.description: Automatically advance to the next logical step in the GSD workflow

name: gsd:note
description_raw: Zero-friction idea capture. Append, list, or promote notes to todos. Three subcommands: append (default), list, promote.
description_normalized: Zero-friction timestamped note capture with list and promote-to-todo subcommands.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:note
frontmatter_yaml.description: Zero-friction idea capture. Append, list, or promote notes to todos.

name: gsd:pause-work
description_raw: Create context handoff when pausing work mid-phase. Creates .continue-here.md handoff file to preserve complete work state across sessions.
description_normalized: Creates a .continue-here.md context handoff file to preserve work state across session pauses.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:pause-work
frontmatter_yaml.description: Create context handoff when pausing work mid-phase

name: gsd:plan-milestone-gaps
description_raw: Create phases to close all gaps identified by milestone audit. Reads MILESTONE-AUDIT.md, groups gaps into logical phases, creates phase entries in ROADMAP.md, and offers to plan each phase.
description_normalized: Creates ROADMAP phases to close all gaps identified by /gsd-audit-milestone.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:plan-milestone-gaps
frontmatter_yaml.description: Create phases to close all gaps identified by milestone audit

name: gsd:plan-phase
description_raw: Create detailed phase plan (PLAN.md) with verification loop. Orchestrates: 4 parallel phase-researcher agents → research synthesizer → planner → plan-checker (loop ≤3). Supports --auto, --research, --skip-research, --gaps, --skip-verify, --prd, --reviews, --text flags.
description_normalized: Orchestrates parallel research, planning, and verification to produce a PLAN.md for a phase.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:plan-phase
frontmatter_yaml.description: Create detailed phase plan (PLAN.md) with verification loop

name: gsd:plant-seed
description_raw: Capture a forward-looking idea with trigger conditions — surfaces automatically at the right milestone. Seeds solve context rot: instead of a one-liner in Deferred that nobody acts on, seeds carry full context and trigger conditions.
description_normalized: Captures a forward-looking idea with trigger conditions that surfaces automatically at the right milestone.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:plant-seed
frontmatter_yaml.description: Capture a forward-looking idea with trigger conditions — surfaces automatically at the right milestone

name: gsd:pr-branch
description_raw: Create a clean PR branch by filtering out .planning/ commits — ready for code review. Solves the problem of PR diffs being cluttered with PLAN.md, SUMMARY.md, STATE.md artifacts.
description_normalized: Creates a clean PR branch by cherry-picking commits that exclude .planning/ artifacts.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:pr-branch
frontmatter_yaml.description: Create a clean PR branch by filtering out .planning/ commits — ready for code review

name: gsd:profile-user
description_raw: Generate developer behavioral profile and create Claude-discoverable artifacts. Analyzes session messages across 8 behavioral dimensions via spawned gsd-user-profiler agent.
description_normalized: Generates a Claude-discoverable developer behavioral profile by spawning gsd-user-profiler to analyze session messages.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:profile-user
frontmatter_yaml.description: Generate developer behavioral profile and create Claude-discoverable artifacts

name: gsd:progress
description_raw: Check project progress, show context, and route to next action (execute or plan). Provides situational awareness before continuing work.
description_normalized: Shows project progress summary and routes to the next action (execute existing plan or create a new one).

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:progress
frontmatter_yaml.description: Check project progress, show context, and route to next action (execute or plan)

name: gsd:quick
description_raw: Execute a quick task with GSD guarantees (atomic commits, state tracking) but skip optional agents. Flags: --full (enable all agents), --validate (add verification), --discuss (enable discussion), --research (enable research).
description_normalized: Executes a quick task with GSD guarantees (atomic commits, state tracking) while skipping optional agent overhead.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:quick
frontmatter_yaml.description: Execute a quick task with GSD guarantees (atomic commits, state tracking) but skip optional agents

name: gsd:reapply-patches
description_raw: Reapply local modifications after a GSD update. Uses three-way comparison (pristine baseline, user-modified backup, newly installed version) to reliably distinguish user customizations from version drift. Critical invariant: never conclude 'no custom content' for any backed-up file.
description_normalized: Reapplies user customizations after a GSD update using three-way comparison to distinguish custom content from version drift.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:reapply-patches
frontmatter_yaml.description: Reapply local modifications after a GSD update
frontmatter_yaml.allowed-tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Glob, Grep, AskUserQuestion

name: gsd:remove-phase
description_raw: Remove a future phase from roadmap and renumber subsequent phases. Clean removal of work you've decided not to do, without polluting context with cancelled/deferred markers.
description_normalized: Removes an unstarted phase from the roadmap and renumbers subsequent phases cleanly.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:remove-phase
frontmatter_yaml.description: Remove a future phase from roadmap and renumber subsequent phases

name: gsd:remove-workspace
description_raw: Remove a GSD workspace and clean up worktrees.
description_normalized: Removes a GSD workspace and cleans up associated git worktrees.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:remove-workspace
frontmatter_yaml.description: Remove a GSD workspace and clean up worktrees

name: gsd:research-phase
description_raw: Research how to implement a phase (standalone — usually use /gsd-plan-phase instead). Spawns gsd-phase-researcher agent with phase context. Note: standalone; plan-phase integrates research automatically.
description_normalized: Standalone command that spawns gsd-phase-researcher to research phase implementation (normally invoked by plan-phase).

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:research-phase

name: gsd:resume-work

name: gstack
description_raw:
Fast headless browser for QA testing and site dogfooding. Navigate pages, interact with
elements, verify state, diff before/after, take annotated screenshots, test responsive
layouts, forms, uploads, dialogs, and capture bug evidence. Use when asked to open or
test a site, verify a deployment, dogfood a user flow, or file a bug with screenshots. (gstack)
description_normalized: Fast headless browser for QA testing and site dogfooding. Navigate pages, interact with

name: autoplan
description_raw:
Auto-review pipeline — reads the full CEO, design, eng, and DX review skills from disk
and runs them sequentially with auto-decisions using 6 decision principles. Surfaces
taste decisions (close approaches, borderline scope, codex disagreements) at a final
approval gate. One command, fully reviewed plan out.
Use when asked to "auto review", "autoplan", "run all reviews", "review this plan
automatically", or "make the decisions for me".
Proactively suggest when the user has a plan file and wants to run the full review
gauntlet without answering 15-30 intermediate questions. (gstack)
description_normalized: Auto-review pipeline — reads the full CEO, design, eng, and DX review skills from disk

name: benchmark
description_raw:
Performance regression detection using the browse daemon. Establishes
baselines for page load times, Core Web Vitals, and resource sizes.
Compares before/after on every PR. Tracks performance trends over time.
Use when: "performance", "benchmark", "page speed", "lighthouse", "web vitals",
"bundle size", "load time". (gstack)
description_normalized: Performance regression detection using the browse daemon. Establishes

name: benchmark-models
description_raw:
Cross-model benchmark for gstack skills. Runs the same prompt through Claude,
GPT (via Codex CLI), and Gemini side-by-side — compares latency, tokens, cost,
and optionally quality via LLM judge. Answers "which model is actually best
for this skill?" with data instead of vibes. Separate from /benchmark, which
measures web page performance. Use when: "benchmark models", "compare models",
"which model is best for X", "cross-model comparison", "model shootout". (gstack)
description_normalized: Cross-model benchmark for gstack skills. Runs the same prompt through Claude,

name: browse
description_raw:
Fast headless browser for QA testing and site dogfooding. Navigate any URL, interact with
elements, verify page state, diff before/after actions, take annotated screenshots, check
responsive layouts, test forms and uploads, handle dialogs, and assert element states.
~100ms per command. Use when you need to test a feature, verify a deployment, dogfood a
user flow, or file a bug with evidence. Use when asked to "open in browser", "test the
site", "take a screenshot", or "dogfood this". (gstack)
description_normalized: Fast headless browser for QA testing and site dogfooding. Navigate any URL, interact with

name: canary
description_raw:
Post-deploy canary monitoring. Watches the live app for console errors,
performance regressions, and page failures using the browse daemon. Takes
periodic screenshots, compares against pre-deploy baselines, and alerts
on anomalies. Use when: "monitor deploy", "canary", "post-deploy check",
"watch production", "verify deploy". (gstack)
description_normalized: Post-deploy canary monitoring. Watches the live app for console errors,

name: careful
description_raw:
Safety guardrails for destructive commands. Warns before rm -rf, DROP TABLE,
force-push, git reset --hard, kubectl delete, and similar destructive operations.
User can override each warning. Use when touching prod, debugging live systems,
or working in a shared environment. Use when asked to "be careful", "safety mode",
"prod mode", or "careful mode". (gstack)
description_normalized: Safety guardrails for destructive commands. Warns before rm -rf, DROP TABLE,

name: claude
description_raw:
Claude Code CLI wrapper for non-Claude hosts - three modes. Review: independent
diff review via claude -p. Challenge: adversarial failure-mode review. Consult:
ask Claude about the repo with read-only file tools. Use when asked for "claude
review", "claude challenge", "ask claude", "second opinion from claude", or
"outside voice". (gstack)
description_normalized: Claude Code CLI wrapper for non-Claude hosts - three modes. Review: independent

name: codex
description_raw:
OpenAI Codex CLI wrapper — three modes. Code review: independent diff review via
codex review with pass/fail gate. Challenge: adversarial mode that tries to break
your code. Consult: ask codex anything with session continuity for follow-ups.
The "200 IQ autistic developer" second opinion. Use when asked to "codex review",
"codex challenge", "ask codex", "second opinion", or "consult codex". (gstack)
description_normalized: OpenAI Codex CLI wrapper — three modes. Code review: independent diff review via

name: context-restore
description_raw:
Restore working context saved earlier by /context-save. Loads the most recent
saved state (across all branches by default) so you can pick up where you
left off — even across Conductor workspace handoffs.
Use when asked to "resume", "restore context", "where was I", or
"pick up where I left off". Pair with /context-save.
Formerly /checkpoint resume — renamed because Claude Code treats /checkpoint
as a native rewind alias in current environments. (gstack)
description_normalized: Restore working context saved earlier by /context-save. Loads the most recent

name: context-save
description_raw:
Save working context. Captures git state, decisions made, and remaining work
so any future session can pick up without losing a beat.
Use when asked to "save progress", "save state", "context save", or
"save my work". Pair with /context-restore to resume later.
Formerly /checkpoint — renamed because Claude Code treats /checkpoint as a
native rewind alias in current environments, which was shadowing this skill.
(gstack)
description_normalized: Save working context. Captures git state, decisions made, and remaining work

name: gstack-contrib-add-host

Contributor-only skill: create a new host config for gstack's multi-host system.
NOT installed for end users. Only usable from the gstack source repo.
description_normalized: Contributor-only skill: create a new host config for gstack's multi-host system.

name: cso
description_raw:
Chief Security Officer mode. Infrastructure-first security audit: secrets archaeology,
dependency supply chain, CI/CD pipeline security, LLM/AI security, skill supply chain
scanning, plus OWASP Top 10, STRIDE threat modeling, and active verification.
Two modes: daily (zero-noise, 8/10 confidence gate) and comprehensive (monthly deep
scan, 2/10 bar). Trend tracking across audit runs.
Use when: "security audit", "threat model", "pentest review", "OWASP", "CSO review". (gstack)
description_normalized: Chief Security Officer mode. Infrastructure-first security audit: secrets archaeology,

name: design-consultation
description_raw:
Design consultation: understands your product, researches the landscape, proposes a
complete design system (aesthetic, typography, color, layout, spacing, motion), and
generates font+color preview pages. Creates DESIGN.md as your project's design source
of truth. For existing sites, use /plan-design-review to infer the system instead.
Use when asked to "design system", "brand guidelines", or "create DESIGN.md".
Proactively suggest when starting a new project's UI with no existing
design system or DESIGN.md. (gstack)
description_normalized: Design consultation: understands your product, researches the landscape, proposes a

name: design-html
description_raw:
Design finalization: generates production-quality Pretext-native HTML/CSS.
Works with approved mockups from /design-shotgun, CEO plans from /plan-ceo-review,
design review context from /plan-design-review, or from scratch with a user
description. Text actually reflows, heights are computed, layouts are dynamic.
30KB overhead, zero deps. Smart API routing: picks the right Pretext patterns
for each design type. Use when: "finalize this design", "turn this into HTML",
"build me a page", "implement this design", or after any planning skill.
Proactively suggest when user has approved a design or has a plan ready. (gstack)
description_normalized: Design finalization: generates production-quality Pretext-native HTML/CSS.

name: design-review
description_raw:
Designer's eye QA: finds visual inconsistency, spacing issues, hierarchy problems,
AI slop patterns, and slow interactions — then fixes them. Iteratively fixes issues
in source code, committing each fix atomically and re-verifying with before/after
screenshots. For plan-mode design review (before implementation), use /plan-design-review.
Use when asked to "audit the design", "visual QA", "check if it looks good", or "design polish".
Proactively suggest when the user mentions visual inconsistencies or
wants to polish the look of a live site. (gstack)
description_normalized: Designer's eye QA: finds visual inconsistency, spacing issues, hierarchy problems,

name: design-shotgun
description_raw:
Design shotgun: generate multiple AI design variants, open a comparison board,
collect structured feedback, and iterate. Standalone design exploration you can
run anytime. Use when: "explore designs", "show me options", "design variants",
"visual brainstorm", or "I don't like how this looks".
Proactively suggest when the user describes a UI feature but hasn't seen
what it could look like. (gstack)
description_normalized: Design shotgun: generate multiple AI design variants, open a comparison board,

name: devex-review
description_raw:
Live developer experience audit. Uses the browse tool to actually TEST the
developer experience: navigates docs, tries the getting started flow, times
TTHW, screenshots error messages, evaluates CLI help text. Produces a DX
scorecard with evidence. Compares against /plan-devex-review scores if they
exist (the boomerang: plan said 3 minutes, reality says 8). Use when asked to
"test the DX", "DX audit", "developer experience test", or "try the
onboarding". Proactively suggest after shipping a developer-facing feature. (gstack)
description_normalized: Live developer experience audit. Uses the browse tool to actually TEST the

name: document-generate
description_raw:
Generate missing documentation from scratch for a feature, module, or entire project.
Uses the Diataxis framework (tutorial / how-to / reference / explanation) to produce
complete, structured documentation. Can be invoked standalone or called by
/document-release when it finds coverage gaps. Use when asked to "write docs",
"generate documentation", "document this feature", "create a tutorial", or
"explain this module". (gstack)
description_normalized: Generate missing documentation from scratch for a feature, module, or entire project.

name: document-release
description_raw:
Post-ship documentation update. Reads all project docs, cross-references the
diff, builds a Diataxis coverage map (reference/how-to/tutorial/explanation),
updates README/ARCHITECTURE/CONTRIBUTING/CLAUDE.md to match what shipped,
detects architecture diagram drift, polishes CHANGELOG voice with a sell-test
rubric, cleans up TODOS, and optionally bumps VERSION. Surfaces documentation
debt in the PR body. Use when asked to "update the docs", "sync documentation",
or "post-ship docs". Proactively suggest after a PR is merged or code is shipped. (gstack)
description_normalized: Post-ship documentation update. Reads all project docs, cross-references the

name: freeze
description_raw:
Restrict file edits to a specific directory for the session. Blocks Edit and
Write outside the allowed path. Use when debugging to prevent accidentally
"fixing" unrelated code, or when you want to scope changes to one module.
Use when asked to "freeze", "restrict edits", "only edit this folder",
or "lock down edits". (gstack)
description_normalized: Restrict file edits to a specific directory for the session. Blocks Edit and

name: gstack-upgrade
description_raw:
Upgrade gstack to the latest version. Detects global vs vendored install,
runs the upgrade, and shows what's new. Use when asked to "upgrade gstack",
"update gstack", or "get latest version".
description_normalized: Upgrade gstack to the latest version. Detects global vs vendored install,

name: guard
description_raw:
Full safety mode: destructive command warnings + directory-scoped edits.
Combines /careful (warns before rm -rf, DROP TABLE, force-push, etc.) with
/freeze (blocks edits outside a specified directory). Use for maximum safety
when touching prod or debugging live systems. Use when asked to "guard mode",
"full safety", "lock it down", or "maximum safety". (gstack)
description_normalized: Full safety mode: destructive command warnings + directory-scoped edits.

name: health
description_raw:
Code quality dashboard. Wraps existing project tools (type checker, linter,
test runner, dead code detector, shell linter), computes a weighted composite
0-10 score, and tracks trends over time. Use when: "health check",
"code quality", "how healthy is the codebase", "run all checks",
"quality score". (gstack)
description_normalized: Code quality dashboard. Wraps existing project tools (type checker, linter,

name: investigate
description_raw:
Systematic debugging with root cause investigation. Four phases: investigate,
analyze, hypothesize, implement. Iron Law: no fixes without root cause.
Use when asked to "debug this", "fix this bug", "why is this broken",
"investigate this error", or "root cause analysis".
Proactively invoke this skill (do NOT debug directly) when the user reports
errors, 500 errors, stack traces, unexpected behavior, "it was working
yesterday", or is troubleshooting why something stopped working. (gstack)
description_normalized: Systematic debugging with root cause investigation. Four phases: investigate,

name: land-and-deploy
description_raw:
Land and deploy workflow. Merges the PR, waits for CI and deploy,
verifies production health via canary checks. Takes over after /ship
creates the PR. Use when: "merge", "land", "deploy", "merge and verify",
"land it", "ship it to production". (gstack)
description_normalized: Land and deploy workflow. Merges the PR, waits for CI and deploy,

name: landing-report
description_raw:
Read-only queue dashboard for workspace-aware ship. Shows which VERSION slots
are currently claimed by open PRs, which sibling Conductor workspaces have
WIP work likely to ship soon, and what slot /ship would pick next. No
mutations — just a snapshot. Use when asked to "landing report", "what's in
the queue", "show me open PRs", or "which version do I claim next". (gstack)
description_normalized: Read-only queue dashboard for workspace-aware ship. Shows which VERSION slots

name: learn
description_raw:
Manage project learnings. Review, search, prune, and export what gstack
has learned across sessions. Use when asked to "what have we learned",
"show learnings", "prune stale learnings", or "export learnings".
Proactively suggest when the user asks about past patterns or wonders
"didn't we fix this before?"
description_normalized: Manage project learnings. Review, search, prune, and export what gstack

name: make-pdf
description_raw:
Turn any markdown file into a publication-quality PDF. Proper 1in margins,
intelligent page breaks, page numbers, cover pages, running headers, curly
quotes and em dashes, clickable TOC, diagonal DRAFT watermark. Not a draft
artifact — a finished artifact. Use when asked to "make a PDF", "export to
PDF", "turn this markdown into a PDF", or "generate a document". (gstack)
description_normalized: Turn any markdown file into a publication-quality PDF. Proper 1in margins,

name: office-hours
description_raw:
YC Office Hours — two modes. Startup mode: six forcing questions that expose
demand reality, status quo, desperate specificity, narrowest wedge, observation,
and future-fit. Builder mode: design thinking brainstorming for side projects,
hackathons, learning, and open source. Saves a design doc.
Use when asked to "brainstorm this", "I have an idea", "help me think through
this", "office hours", or "is this worth building".
Proactively invoke this skill (do NOT answer directly) when the user describes
a new product idea, asks whether something is worth building, wants to think
through design decisions for something that doesn't exist yet, or is exploring
a concept before any code is written.
Use before /plan-ceo-review or /plan-eng-review. (gstack)
description_normalized: YC Office Hours — two modes. Startup mode: six forcing questions that expose

name: open-gstack-browser
description_raw:
Launch GStack Browser — AI-controlled Chromium with the sidebar extension baked in.
Opens a visible browser window where you can watch every action in real time.
The sidebar shows a live activity feed and chat. Anti-bot stealth built in.
Use when asked to "open gstack browser", "launch browser", "connect chrome",
"open chrome", "real browser", "launch chrome", "side panel", or "control my browser".
description_normalized: Launch GStack Browser — AI-controlled Chromium with the sidebar extension baked in.

name: pair-agent
description_raw:
Pair a remote AI agent with your browser. One command generates a setup key and
prints instructions the other agent can follow to connect. Works with OpenClaw,
Hermes, Codex, Cursor, or any agent that can make HTTP requests. The remote agent
gets its own tab with scoped access (read+write by default, admin on request).
Use when asked to "pair agent", "connect agent", "share browser", "remote browser",
"let another agent use my browser", or "give browser access". (gstack)
description_normalized: Pair a remote AI agent with your browser. One command generates a setup key and

name: plan-ceo-review
description_raw:
CEO/founder-mode plan review. Rethink the problem, find the 10-star product,
challenge premises, expand scope when it creates a better product. Four modes:
SCOPE EXPANSION (dream big), SELECTIVE EXPANSION (hold scope + cherry-pick
expansions), HOLD SCOPE (maximum rigor), SCOPE REDUCTION (strip to essentials).
Use when asked to "think bigger", "expand scope", "strategy review", "rethink this",
or "is this ambitious enough".
Proactively suggest when the user is questioning scope or ambition of a plan,
or when the plan feels like it could be thinking bigger. (gstack)
description_normalized: CEO/founder-mode plan review. Rethink the problem, find the 10-star product,

name: plan-design-review
description_raw:
Designer's eye plan review — interactive, like CEO and Eng review.
Rates each design dimension 0-10, explains what would make it a 10,
then fixes the plan to get there. Works in plan mode. For live site
visual audits, use /design-review. Use when asked to "review the design plan"
or "design critique".
Proactively suggest when the user has a plan with UI/UX components that
should be reviewed before implementation. (gstack)
description_normalized: Designer's eye plan review — interactive, like CEO and Eng review.

name: plan-devex-review
description_raw:
Interactive developer experience plan review. Explores developer personas,
benchmarks against competitors, designs magical moments, and traces friction
points before scoring. Three modes: DX EXPANSION (competitive advantage),
DX POLISH (bulletproof every touchpoint), DX TRIAGE (critical gaps only).
Use when asked to "DX review", "developer experience audit", "devex review",
or "API design review".
Proactively suggest when the user has a plan for developer-facing products
(APIs, CLIs, SDKs, libraries, platforms, docs). (gstack)
description_normalized: Interactive developer experience plan review. Explores developer personas,

name: plan-eng-review
description_raw:
Eng manager-mode plan review. Lock in the execution plan — architecture,
data flow, diagrams, edge cases, test coverage, performance. Walks through
issues interactively with opinionated recommendations. Use when asked to
"review the architecture", "engineering review", or "lock in the plan".
Proactively suggest when the user has a plan or design doc and is about to
start coding — to catch architecture issues before implementation. (gstack)
description_normalized: Eng manager-mode plan review. Lock in the execution plan — architecture,

name: plan-tune
description_raw:
Self-tuning question sensitivity + developer psychographic for gstack (v1: observational).
Review which AskUserQuestion prompts fire across gstack skills, set per-question preferences
(never-ask / always-ask / ask-only-for-one-way), inspect the dual-track
profile (what you declared vs what your behavior suggests), and enable/disable
question tuning. Conversational interface — no CLI syntax required.
description_normalized: Self-tuning question sensitivity + developer psychographic for gstack (v1: observational).

name: qa
description_raw:
Systematically QA test a web application and fix bugs found. Runs QA testing,
then iteratively fixes bugs in source code, committing each fix atomically and
re-verifying. Use when asked to "qa", "QA", "test this site", "find bugs",
"test and fix", or "fix what's broken".
Proactively suggest when the user says a feature is ready for testing
or asks "does this work?". Three tiers: Quick (critical/high only),
Standard (+ medium), Exhaustive (+ cosmetic). Produces before/after health scores,
fix evidence, and a ship-readiness summary. For report-only mode, use /qa-only. (gstack)
description_normalized: Systematically QA test a web application and fix bugs found. Runs QA testing,

name: qa-only
description_raw:
Report-only QA testing. Systematically tests a web application and produces a
structured report with health score, screenshots, and repro steps — but never
fixes anything. Use when asked to "just report bugs", "qa report only", or
"test but don't fix". For the full test-fix-verify loop, use /qa instead.
Proactively suggest when the user wants a bug report without any code changes. (gstack)
description_normalized: Report-only QA testing. Systematically tests a web application and produces a

name: retro
description_raw:
Weekly engineering retrospective. Analyzes commit history, work patterns,
and code quality metrics with persistent history and trend tracking.
Team-aware: breaks down per-person contributions with praise and growth areas.
Use when asked to "weekly retro", "what did we ship", or "engineering retrospective".
Proactively suggest at the end of a work week or sprint. (gstack)
description_normalized: Weekly engineering retrospective. Analyzes commit history, work patterns,

name: review
description_raw:
Pre-landing PR review. Analyzes diff against the base branch for SQL safety, LLM trust
boundary violations, conditional side effects, and other structural issues. Use when
asked to "review this PR", "code review", "pre-landing review", or "check my diff".
Proactively suggest when the user is about to merge or land code changes. (gstack)
description_normalized: Pre-landing PR review. Analyzes diff against the base branch for SQL safety, LLM trust

name: scrape
description_raw:
Pull data from a web page. First call on a new intent prototypes the flow
via $B primitives and returns JSON. Subsequent calls on a matching intent
route to a codified browser-skill and return in ~200ms. Read-only — for
mutating flows (form fills, clicks, submissions), use /automate.
Use when asked to "scrape", "get data from", "pull", "extract from", or
"what's on" a page. (gstack)
description_normalized: Pull data from a web page. First call on a new intent prototypes the flow

name: setup-browser-cookies
description_raw:
Import cookies from your real Chromium browser into the headless browse session.
Opens an interactive picker UI where you select which cookie domains to import.
Use before QA testing authenticated pages. Use when asked to "import cookies",
"login to the site", or "authenticate the browser". (gstack)
description_normalized: Import cookies from your real Chromium browser into the headless browse session.

name: setup-deploy
description_raw:
Configure deployment settings for /land-and-deploy. Detects your deploy
platform (Fly.io, Render, Vercel, Netlify, Heroku, GitHub Actions, custom),
production URL, health check endpoints, and deploy status commands. Writes
the configuration to CLAUDE.md so all future deploys are automatic.
Use when: "setup deploy", "configure deployment", "set up land-and-deploy",
"how do I deploy with gstack", "add deploy config".
description_normalized: Configure deployment settings for /land-and-deploy. Detects your deploy

name: setup-gbrain
description_raw:
Set up gbrain for this coding agent: install the CLI, initialize a
local PGLite or Supabase brain, register MCP, capture per-remote trust
policy. One command from zero to "gbrain is running, and this agent
can call it." Use when: "setup gbrain", "connect gbrain", "start
gbrain", "install gbrain", "configure gbrain for this machine". (gstack)
description_normalized: Set up gbrain for this coding agent: install the CLI, initialize a

name: ship
description_raw:
Ship workflow: detect + merge base branch, run tests, review diff, bump VERSION,
update CHANGELOG, commit, push, create PR. Use when asked to "ship", "deploy",
"push to main", "create a PR", "merge and push", or "get it deployed".
Proactively invoke this skill (do NOT push/PR directly) when the user says code
is ready, asks about deploying, wants to push code up, or asks to create a PR. (gstack)
description_normalized: Ship workflow: detect + merge base branch, run tests, review diff, bump VERSION,

name: skillify
description_raw:
Codify the most recent successful /scrape flow into a permanent
browser-skill on disk. Future /scrape calls with the same intent run
the codified script in ~200ms instead of re-driving the page. Walks
back through the conversation, synthesizes script.ts + script.test.ts
+ fixture, runs the test in a temp dir, and asks before committing.
Use when asked to "skillify", "codify", "save this scrape", or
"make this permanent". (gstack)
description_normalized: Codify the most recent successful /scrape flow into a permanent

name: sync-gbrain
description_raw:
Keep gbrain current with this repo's code and refresh agent search
guidance in CLAUDE.md. Wraps the gstack-gbrain-sync orchestrator with
state probing, native code-surface registration, capability checks,
and a verdict block. Re-runnable, idempotent. Use when: "sync gbrain",
"refresh gbrain", "re-index this repo", "gbrain search isn't finding
things". (gstack)
description_normalized: Keep gbrain current with this repo's code and refresh agent search

name: unfreeze
description_raw:
Clear the freeze boundary set by /freeze, allowing edits to all directories
again. Use when you want to widen edit scope without ending the session.
Use when asked to "unfreeze", "unlock edits", "remove freeze", or
"allow all edits". (gstack)
description_normalized: Clear the freeze boundary set by /freeze, allowing edits to all directories

name: penny
description_raw: Expert Prompt Engineer & Template Architect — methodical, quality-focused, educational. Walks users through the full prompt lifecycle: create Handlebars template + JSON schema (*new), refine iteratively (*refine), test with mock data (*test), validate structure (*validate). Uses POEM-principled workflows. Dependencies: 4 workflows, 3 data files, 3 skills.
description_normalized: POEM prompt engineer agent that guides users through the full prompt lifecycle — create, refine, test, and validate Handlebars templates with JSON schemas.

name: alex
description_raw: Workflow Architect & Orchestration Designer — conversational, analytical, design-focused. Interviews users to understand orchestration goals, scans existing prompts and schemas to build an I/O inventory (activation Step 4), maps data flow, and generates clean workflow YAML with human checkpoints plus a companion .usage.md guide. Commands: *design, *analyze, *generate, *validate, *refine. Dependencies: 5 workflows, 3 data files, 3 skills.
description_normalized: POEM workflow architect agent that interviews users, scans existing prompts for I/O contracts, and generates orchestrating workflow YAML with companion usage guides.
