name: brainstorming
description_raw: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation. Guides a structured spec-and-design dialogue: one question at a time, 2-3 approach proposals, design approval gate, write design doc, spec self-review, then hand off to writing-plans.
description_normalized: Structured design dialogue skill that turns ideas into approved spec documents before any implementation begins.

frontmatter_yaml.name: brainstorming
frontmatter_yaml.description: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.

name: dispatching-parallel-agents
description_raw: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies. Delegates each independent problem domain to a focused subagent with precisely crafted context, runs them concurrently, then reviews and integrates the results.
description_normalized: Parallel subagent dispatch skill for independent tasks that can run concurrently without shared state.

frontmatter_yaml.name: dispatching-parallel-agents
frontmatter_yaml.description: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies

name: executing-plans
description_raw: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints. Loads plan, reviews critically, executes all tasks following each step exactly, stops and asks on blockers, then hands off to finishing-a-development-branch.
description_normalized: Sequential plan execution skill for running a written implementation plan task-by-task with blocking checkpoints.

frontmatter_yaml.name: executing-plans
frontmatter_yaml.description: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints

name: finishing-a-development-branch
description_raw: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup. Verifies tests, detects environment (worktree vs normal repo, detached HEAD), presents exactly 4 or 3 structured options, executes choice, cleans up workspace with provenance check.
description_normalized: Branch completion skill that verifies tests and presents structured merge/PR/keep/discard options with safe cleanup.

frontmatter_yaml.name: finishing-a-development-branch
frontmatter_yaml.description: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup

name: receiving-code-review
description_raw: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation. Enforces: read, understand, verify, evaluate, respond technically, implement one item at a time.
description_normalized: Code review reception skill that enforces technical verification over performative agreement before implementing any feedback.

frontmatter_yaml.name: receiving-code-review
frontmatter_yaml.description: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation

name: requesting-code-review
description_raw: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements. Dispatches a code reviewer subagent with precisely crafted context (git SHAs, description, requirements) to catch issues before they cascade. Mandatory after each task in subagent-driven development.
description_normalized: Code review request skill that dispatches a focused reviewer subagent with crafted context after each implementation task.

frontmatter_yaml.name: requesting-code-review
frontmatter_yaml.description: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements

name: subagent-driven-development
description_raw: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session. Dispatches fresh implementer subagent per task (receives full task text, never reads plan file), followed by two-stage review: spec compliance reviewer then code quality reviewer. Continuous execution — no check-ins between tasks. Ends with final code review and finishing-a-development-branch.
description_normalized: Continuous plan execution skill using fresh subagents per task with mandatory two-stage spec-then-quality review gates.

frontmatter_yaml.name: subagent-driven-development
frontmatter_yaml.description: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session

name: systematic-debugging
description_raw: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes. Iron Law: NO FIXES WITHOUT ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION FIRST. Four phases: root cause investigation (gather evidence), pattern analysis (find working examples), hypothesis and testing (one variable at a time), implementation (failing test first, then fix). After 3+ failed fixes, question the architecture.
description_normalized: Systematic root-cause debugging skill that enforces evidence-first investigation through four mandatory phases before any fix.

frontmatter_yaml.name: systematic-debugging
frontmatter_yaml.description: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes

name: test-driven-development
description_raw: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code. Iron Law: NO PRODUCTION CODE WITHOUT A FAILING TEST FIRST. Red-Green-Refactor cycle: write failing test, verify it fails for the right reason, write minimal code to pass, verify passes, refactor. No exceptions — delete code written before tests and start over. Includes Rationalizations table of verbatim agent excuses.
description_normalized: Strict TDD skill enforcing test-first development with Iron Law, Red-Green-Refactor cycle, and pre-emptive rationalization counters.

frontmatter_yaml.name: test-driven-development
frontmatter_yaml.description: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code

name: using-git-worktrees
description_raw: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - ensures an isolated workspace exists via native tools or git worktree fallback. Detects existing isolation first, defers to native harness tools, falls back to manual git worktree with safety verification, then runs project setup and clean baseline test.
description_normalized: Workspace isolation skill that detects existing worktrees, defers to native harness tools, and falls back to git worktrees with safety checks.

frontmatter_yaml.name: using-git-worktrees
frontmatter_yaml.description: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - ensures an isolated workspace exists via native tools or git worktree fallback

name: using-superpowers
description_raw: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions. Even 1% chance a skill applies = must invoke it. Includes Red Flags rationalization table, skill priority order, and SUBAGENT-STOP guard. This skill body is injected at session start via hooks/session-start bootstrap.
description_normalized: Session bootstrap skill that enforces mandatory skill invocation before any action, with anti-rationalization guards.

frontmatter_yaml.name: using-superpowers
frontmatter_yaml.description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions

name: verification-before-completion
description_raw: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always. Iron Law: NO COMPLETION CLAIMS WITHOUT FRESH VERIFICATION EVIDENCE. Gate function: identify command, run it fresh, read full output, verify, then claim. Includes Rationalization Prevention table.
description_normalized: Verification gate skill that requires fresh command evidence before any completion claim, commit, or PR creation.

frontmatter_yaml.name: verification-before-completion
frontmatter_yaml.description: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always

name: writing-plans
description_raw: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code. Writes comprehensive implementation plans: file structure map first, then bite-sized tasks (2-5 min steps) with exact file paths, complete code, exact commands and expected output. No placeholders. Every plan includes required REQUIRED SUB-SKILL header. Ends with execution handoff choice (subagent-driven vs inline).
description_normalized: Implementation plan authoring skill that produces zero-placeholder, TDD-structured, bite-sized task plans with complete code at each step.

frontmatter_yaml.name: writing-plans
frontmatter_yaml.description: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code

name: writing-skills
description_raw: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment. Applies TDD to skill authoring: RED (run baseline pressure scenario, document verbatim rationalizations), GREEN (write minimal SKILL.md addressing those specific failures), REFACTOR (close loopholes, rebuild rationalization table, re-test). Iron Law: NO SKILL WITHOUT A FAILING TEST FIRST. Includes Claude Search Optimization (CSO) — description = trigger only, never workflow summary.
description_normalized: Skill authoring meta-skill applying TDD Red-Green-Refactor to documentation: baseline pressure testing before writing, rationalization tables, CSO-compliant descriptions.

frontmatter_yaml.name: writing-skills
frontmatter_yaml.description: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment

name: diagnose
description_raw: Disciplined diagnosis loop for hard bugs and performance regressions. Reproduce → minimise → hypothesise → instrument → fix → regression-test. Use when user says "diagnose this" / "debug this", reports a bug, says something is broken/throwing/failing, or describes a performance regression.
description_normalized: Six-phase debugging loop (reproduce → minimise → hypothesise → instrument → fix → regression-test) with feedback-loop construction as the mandatory first phase.

frontmatter_yaml.name: diagnose
frontmatter_yaml.description: Disciplined diagnosis loop for hard bugs and performance regressions.

notes: feedback-loop-first is a candidate pattern not yet in vocabulary. Supporting files in skill dir (not fully inspected via remote).

name: grill-with-docs
description_raw: Grilling session that challenges your plan against the existing domain model, sharpens terminology, and updates documentation (CONTEXT.md, ADRs) inline as decisions crystallise. Use when user wants to stress-test a plan against their project's language and documented decisions.
description_normalized: Interrogative alignment session that sharpens domain vocabulary, cross-references code against CONTEXT.md, and writes glossary entries and ADRs inline as decisions land.

frontmatter_yaml.name: grill-with-docs
frontmatter_yaml.description: Grilling session that challenges your plan against the existing domain model, sharpens terminology, and updates documentation (CONTEXT.md, ADRs) inline as decisions crystallise.

notes: grill-first-alignment and vocabulary-only-context are candidate patterns not yet in vocabulary. Supporting files: CONTEXT-FORMAT.md, ADR-FORMAT.md inside skill dir.

name: triage
description_raw: Triage issues through a state machine of triage roles.
description_normalized: Issue triage skill that drives issues through a labeled state machine of canonical triage roles configured per-repo by setup-matt-pocock-skills.

frontmatter_yaml.name: triage
frontmatter_yaml.description: Triage issues through a state machine of triage roles.

notes: Reads triage label vocabulary from docs/agents/triage-labels.md written by setup-matt-pocock-skills.

name: improve-codebase-architecture
description_raw: Find deepening opportunities in a codebase, informed by the domain language in CONTEXT.md and the decisions in docs/adr/.
description_normalized: Architecture improvement skill that uses the project's domain glossary and ADRs to find module-deepening opportunities (Ousterhout deep modules).

frontmatter_yaml.name: improve-codebase-architecture
frontmatter_yaml.description: Find deepening opportunities in a codebase, informed by the domain language in CONTEXT.md and the decisions in docs/adr/.

notes: Recommended as handoff target from diagnose when no good test seam exists.

name: setup-matt-pocock-skills
description_raw: Scaffold the per-repo config (issue tracker, triage label vocabulary, domain doc layout) that the other engineering skills consume. Run once per repo before using to-issues, to-prd, triage, diagnose, tdd, improve-codebase-architecture, or zoom-out.
description_normalized: Per-repo bootstrap skill that scaffolds issue tracker config, triage label vocabulary, and domain doc layout required by all other engineering skills.

frontmatter_yaml.name: setup-matt-pocock-skills
frontmatter_yaml.description: Scaffold the per-repo config (issue tracker, triage label vocabulary, domain doc layout) that the other engineering skills consume.

notes: Must run before other engineering skills. Analogous to repo-onboard patterns in ECC corpus.

name: tdd
description_raw: Test-driven development with red-green-refactor loop. Use when user wants to build features or fix bugs using TDD, mentions "red-green-refactor", wants integration tests, or asks for test-first development.
description_normalized: TDD skill enforcing vertical-slice tracer-bullet cycles with explicit anti-horizontal-slice doctrine; links Ousterhout deep-modules into test design via in-dir reference files.

frontmatter_yaml.name: tdd
frontmatter_yaml.description: Test-driven development with red-green-refactor loop. Use when user wants to build features or fix bugs using TDD, mentions "red-green-refactor", wants integration tests, or asks for test-first development.

notes: Supporting files in skill dir: tests.md, mocking.md, deep-modules.md, interface-design.md, refactoring.md. Only corpus artifact to explicitly name and prohibit 'horizontal slicing' anti-pattern.

name: caveman
description_raw: Ultra-compressed communication mode. Cuts token usage ~75% by dropping filler, articles, and pleasantries while keeping full technical accuracy. Use when user says "caveman mode", "talk like caveman", "use caveman", "less tokens", "be brief", or invokes /caveman.
description_normalized: Persistent ultra-compressed communication mode dropping filler and articles for ~75% token reduction; preserves technical accuracy and code blocks; Auto-Clarity Exception for destructive operations.

frontmatter_yaml.name: caveman
frontmatter_yaml.description: Ultra-compressed communication mode. Cuts token usage ~75% by dropping filler, articles, and pleasantries while keeping full technical accuracy.

notes: communication-mode is a proposed new cluster — no existing cluster fits. token-compression-mode is a proposed new pattern. Only corpus artifact that installs a persistent named communication style rather than a task workflow.

name: grill-me
description_raw: Interview the user relentlessly about a plan or design until reaching shared understanding, resolving each branch of the decision tree. Use when user wants to stress-test a plan, get grilled on their design, or mentions "grill me".
description_normalized: Alignment interview skill that resolves every branch of a design's decision tree one-at-a-time, with agent-provided recommended answers for each question.

frontmatter_yaml.name: grill-me
frontmatter_yaml.description: Interview the user relentlessly about a plan or design until reaching shared understanding, resolving each branch of the decision tree.

notes: grill-first-alignment is a candidate pattern not yet in vocabulary. Simpler sibling of grill-with-docs — no domain doc integration.

name: handoff
description_raw: Compact the current conversation into a handoff document for another agent to pick up.
description_normalized: Condenses the current conversation into an OS-temp handoff document for a fresh agent, with suggested skills section and sensitive data redacted.

frontmatter_yaml.name: handoff
frontmatter_yaml.description: Compact the current conversation into a handoff document for another agent to pick up.
frontmatter_yaml.argument-hint: What will the next session be used for?

notes: argument-hint frontmatter field — guides user on what argument to pass. Saves to OS temp dir, not workspace. Redacts sensitive data.

name: k-fleet
description_raw: Query the KyberBot agent fleet (k-brains, k-lars, k-kybernesis, k-roam, k-awb, etc.) via their HTTP APIs to recall entities, semantic-search content, query timelines, or check fleet health. Reads ~/.kyberbot/registry.yaml to discover agents (local + remote via Tailscale). Use when user says "k-fleet", "fleet status", "list my agents", "ask k-brains", "ask k-kybernesis", "search my brains", "search the fleet", "what does k-brains know about X", "recall from k-roam", "kyberbot search", "kyberbot recall", "timeline from k-brains this week", or any phrasing that asks to pull memories, entities, or events from a KyberBot agent's persistent graph database. This is the cross-agent equivalent of the local `kyberbot recall/search/timeline` CLI — works from any project directory without cd-ing into an agent folder.

name: archon
description_raw:
|
  Use when: User wants to run Archon workflows, CREATE workflows or commands,
  set up Archon, or manage Archon configuration.
  Triggers (run): "use archon to", "run archon", "archon workflow", "use archon for",
            "have archon", "let archon", "ask archon to".
  Triggers (create): "create a workflow", "write a workflow", "make a command",
            "author a workflow", "new workflow", "new command", "archon workflow yaml".
  Triggers (setup): "set up archon", "install archon", "how to use archon",
            "configure archon", "archon setup", "get started with archon".
  Triggers (config): "change my archon config", "modify archon config", "archon config",
            "change archon settings", "update my config", "help me change my config",
            "edit archon config", "archon configuration".
  Triggers (init): "initialize archon", "set up .archon", "archon init", "add archon to repo".
  Capability: Runs AI workflows in isolated git worktrees for parallel development.
  Also: Creates and manages workflow YAML files, command files, and configuration.
  NOT for: Direct Claude Code work - only for delegating to Archon CLI.

            "have archon"

name: brain-bridge
description_raw: Store specific knowledge into a named second brain collection. Trigger when user says "save to brain", "save to 2nd brain", "save to second brain", "put that in my [brain-name] brain", "save to [brain-name]", "add that to my learnings brain", "store in bmad-poem", "write that to my workflows brain", "add to my tech-stack brain", "save to my projects brain". If the user says "save to brain" or "save to 2nd brain" without specifying which brain, ASK which brain before proceeding — available brains are: bmad-poem, learnings, workflows, tech-stack, projects. Do NOT use for general memory or session continuity — use capture-context for session handover, and Claude's built-in memory for general facts.

Ask about brands, list projects, check status, S3 collaboration, git repos, sharing, and archiving. Use when mentioning video assets, brand content, backup, upload, download, sync, project discovery, or external SSD storage.

name: omi-extract
description_raw:
>
  Extract and classify OMI wearable transcripts with two-tier metadata tagging.
  Use when user says "extract omi", "tag omi transcripts", "classify omi files",
  "enrich omi", "run omi extraction", "process omi recordings", "what are my omi
  transcripts about", "backfill omi tags", "omi summary", "omi extraction stats".
  Writes signal classification, brain routing, people, entities, and routing
  suggestions into each file's YAML frontmatter. Uses Gemini Flash-Lite (free tier).
  Companion to omi-fetch (which retrieves transcripts from the API).

name: omi-extract-haiku
description_raw:
>
  Extract and classify OMI conversation transcripts using Haiku model via the
  Claude Code harness (Max plan credits — no external API, no Gemini quota).
  Spawns parallel Haiku background agents for isolated per-batch classification.
  Adds extraction_model: haiku to distinguish from Gemini-extracted files.
  Use when: 'extract with haiku', 'haiku extract', 'omi haiku', 'intake extract haiku',
  'compare extraction models', 'test haiku on omi files', 'run haiku extractor'.

>
  Extract and classify OMI conversation transcripts using Haiku model via the

  Spawns parallel Haiku background agents for isolated per-batch classification.

name: radar

  who always knew what was happening before anyone asked. Use when user says "hey radar",
  "radar", "morning brief", "what's on radar", "check radar", "what's happening today",
  "what do I have on", "radar add: [task]", or any variation of checking their daily
  task state. Reads the todo brain (~/dev/ad/brains/todo/) and delivers a sharp morning
  briefing. Can also capture new tasks directly into inbox.md.

name: supportsignal-planning-sync
description_raw: Keep the SupportSignal v2 planning repo in sync with its three satellite apps (Signal Studio, AWB, prompt.supportsignal). Use when asked to do a "planning sync", "satellite sync", "weekly sync", "daily sync", "what changed in Signal Studio/AWB", "update planning docs from satellites", "gap audit a satellite", or "run a handover check". Supports two cadences: daily lightweight (git log scan + targeted update) and weekly deep (full gap audit + JSON reconciliation using Ralphy wave pattern).
